item.title: Bad Company: Art and big business collide in new Anne Edmonds, Kitty Flanagan comedy
item.description: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

item.title: Missing 5yo girl believed to have been abducted near Alice Springs, police say
item.description: Northern Territory Police say they believe a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs was abducted from her home at a town camp early on Sunday morning.

item.title: WA Criminal Mental Impairment Act 'impossible' to fully implement in practice, judge says
item.description: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

item.title: Japan's north struck by magnitude-6.1 earthquake, followed by wildfires prompting evacuations
item.description: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

item.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla proceed with US state visit after White House shooting
item.description: The British monarch and Queen Camilla are embarking on a state visit to the United States with relations between the two countries strained and following a shooting at a press gala in Washington.

item.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it
item.description: Sabastian Sawe smashes one of athletics' seemingly unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

item.title: Sheep farmers swear by Lifetime Ewe course to steer them through drought and diesel price spikes
item.description: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

item.title: NT Police seek 47-year-old Jefferson Lewis in relation to missing 5-year-old Alice Springs girl
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police search waterways in Adelaide's south for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano
item.description: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

item.title: Australian Gregory Louden on leading PlayStation's next big title, Saros
item.description: Developer Gregory Louden always wanted to work in games, but it was a sidestep into filmmaking that helped him secure his dream job at Finnish studio Housemarque.

item.title: Who is Cole Tomas Allen, Donald Trump's would-be assassin?
item.description: Details from the life of US President Donald Trump's would-be assassin paint a picture of a high achiever who took an abrupt turn towards political violence.

item.title: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission
item.description: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: Here are the candidates in Tasmania's Legislative Council elections in Huon and Rosevears
item.description: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: Gormandale footballers break 65-game losing streak with victory in North Gippsland league
item.description: Gormandale players are over the moon after breaking a 65-game losing streak on the weekend.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas says algal bloom 'gone' but fishing restrictions need to expand
item.description: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australia due to the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks, but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up".

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: AFL great Mick Malthouse struck alleged intruder in home raid, court hears
item.description: Legendary AFL coach Mick Malthouse "swung an exercise pole" and hit an intruder in the head as he defended his family during an alleged attempted home invasion last year, a Melbourne court has heard.

item.title: What we know about Jefferson Lewis, the man police are searching for over the alleged abduction of a five-year-old girl in Alice Springs
item.description: A man being sought in connection with the alleged abduction of five-year-old Sharon Granites near Alice Springs has a long history of violence and prison time in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Snowfall in April, record hot day in May. Is this a new norm for Tasmania? Scientists explain
item.description: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: RSL Australia to review Welcome to Country policies after elders booed on Anzac Day
item.description: RSL Australia will review its Welcome to Country guidelines after Indigenous elders were booed during Anzac Day services across the country.

item.title: AFL concedes error on controversial ARC call as league boss says umpiring 'as good as it's been for a long time'
item.description: AFL football boss Greg Swann says most of the feedback he receives from the clubs surrounding the officiating in the league is positive despite a pair of controversial review calls in Round 7.

item.title: Thomas Clarke sentenced to 21 years in prison for gruesome Dimboola murder
item.description: Thomas Clarke is sentenced to 21 years in prison for murdering a man in Dimboola in 2023.

item.title: Ex-wife of motorcyclist killed in Yarck hit and run says she hoped to reunite with him
item.description: The County Court hears emotional statements from the ex-wife and daughters of a motorcyclist who was killed by a drug-affected driver in a stolen car in Central Victoria.

item.title: Murder trial hears multiple theories surrounding Gavin Preston's death
item.description: Victoria's Supreme Court hears police received numerous tip-offs after underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead, including claims of a cemetery desecration and a feud with an illegal tobacco kingpin.

item.title: Monday Finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Jury trial of Greyhound bus driver Peter Cafe begins in Townsville
item.description: The jury was told witnesses heard bus driver Peter Jason Cafe say 'I fell asleep' in the aftermath of a bus crash that claimed three lives.

item.title: Taroom Trough's oil field touted as solution to fuel security but no-one knows how much oil is there
item.description: According to Queensland's premier the Taroom Trough could be the solution to averting a future fuel crisis and a once-in-a-generation opportunity to claw back sovereign capability.

item.title: Australia's minimum fuel reserves would double under $800m Coalition plan, at a cost to drivers
item.description: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

item.title: Lebanon and Hezbollah at odds over peace talks with Israel as strikes continue despite ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's president has accused Hezbollah of hypocrisy, as the Iranian-backed militant group demanded the government walk away from peace talks with Israel.

item.title: Press gala shooting suspect charged with attempted assassination of Donald Trump — as it happened
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, is charged with the attempted assassination of the president, a crime that carries a maximum sentence of life in prison. Take a look back at how the day's events unfolded.

item.title: Train collision near Jakarta kills 14 as rescuers complete evacuation
item.description: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

item.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far
item.description: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: Girl's suspected abductor had been out of jail for just six days, police say
item.description: Northern Territory Police believe five-year-old Sharon Granites was abducted from a home in an Alice Springs town camp as the search continues into its third day.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook another casualty as worries about AI's impact on jobs grow
item.description: Tim Cook helped usher in a communications revolution and his departure from Apple marks a turning point for the company. It also serves as a reminder of how AI is changing the tech landscape.

item.title: Health insurer Bupa accused of anti-competitive behaviour under secretive No Gap scheme deals
item.description: Leaked contracts obtained by the ABC reveal Bupa's deals with private hospitals impose stifling conditions on operators that are too small to push back.

item.title: Soaring petrol prices force tourism operators to introduce surcharges
item.description: Fuel costs have more than doubled for one of the Great Barrier Reef's major tourism operators — and it's forcing a rethink on how paradise is powered.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Learn why tomorrow's inflation data could be important for you in just three minutes
item.description: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

item.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
item.description: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

item.title: Saudi Arabia's dazzling vision was crashing into reality even before the Iran war broke out
item.description: Saudi Arabia's plans to transform its economy were already facing headwinds when the Iran war broke out, placing them even further in jeopardy.

item.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
item.description: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

item.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
item.description: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
item.description: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: Australians living in fear as calls grow for inquiry into foreign government harassment
item.description: Iranian Australian journalist and academic Saba Vasefi has spent years documenting human rights abuses in her homeland. The regime she's criticised has not forgotten her name.

item.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
item.description: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

item.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
item.description: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

item.title: Trump greets King Charles at White House
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
item.description: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

item.title: WA digital drivers licence trial sparks cybersecurity concerns from expert
item.description: A cybersecurity expert warns people's privacy and security could be at risk once the state government rolls out its new digital drivers licence initiative.

item.title: NFL kicker Younghoe Koo's comical error revealed a brain tumour in fan Mark Toothaker
item.description: Mark Toothaker laughed so hard at Younghoe Koo's botched NFL kick that he had a seizure. It may have saved his life.

item.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
item.description: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

item.title: Third assassination attempt on Donald Trump highlights US history of political violence
item.description: The latest attack on US President Donald Trump has once again thrust his country's long history of political violence into the spotlight.

item.title: UK parliament's AUKUS inquiry report questions if Britain can keep submarine promises
item.description: The House of Commons Defence Committee releases the findings of its yearlong review into the trilateral defence partnership.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
item.description: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

item.title: Rock climber dies after falling at Organ Pipes near Mount Arapiles
item.description: The woman was climbing with a group of other climbers at Organ Pipes near Mount Arapiles yesterday.

item.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
item.description: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
item.description: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Yarra River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
item.description: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
item.description: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

item.title: Millie Elliott returns after having a baby to line up for NSW in State of Origin Game I
item.description: Millie Elliott could have taken an easy route back into competitive action after having a baby. Instead, she is throwing herself straight back into the State of Origin cauldron on Thursday night.

item.title: Why NSW hasn’t met a problem Tiana Penitani Gray couldn’t solve
item.description: Tiana Penitani Gray has never played second row before, but as the Origin stalwart prepares to play her third position in as many series such a bold change is nothing new.

item.title: Health insurers accused of 'aggressive' and 'almost bullying' tactics in private hospital dealings
item.description: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Alex Johnston and Jarome Luai set to join PNG Chiefs in major get for NRL expansion team
item.description: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

item.title: Mother 'always thinks' about whether birth delay at regional hospital led to son's ongoing health issues
item.description: A regional South Australian mother who faced a range of stressful diversions during her son's birth says she will always wonder whether his serious and ongoing health concerns were caused by delays in receiving care.

item.title: Locusts seen on Riverland farm
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Locust swarms in the Murtho area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
item.description: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

item.title: New police operation investigating crime syndicates 'pulling the strings' in Melbourne firebombings
item.description: Police are investigating whether recent arson attacks across 15 Melbourne hospitality venues are linked to an organised crime syndicate.

item.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago as historians search for murder scene
item.description: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

item.title: David Summers-Smith not criminally responsible for fatally stabbing British tourist
item.description: A man who killed a British tourist in a random stabbing at an Albury motel in 2024 has been found not criminally responsible for the attack.

item.title: Protester who dumped shark heads at minister's door willing to go to jail
item.description: Bunbury fisherman Mason Jones, who led a protest against the WA government's demersal fishing ban, says he has no intention of stopping the campaign.

item.title: Bus driver's trial over fatal Gumlu crash continues in Townsville
item.description: The first police officers to arrive on the scene of a fatal bus crash in north Queensland have told the court of their conversations with the driver in the immediate aftermath of the incident.

item.title: Woman and child allegedly carjacked in Dalyellup after burning dirtbike blocks road
item.description: Police allege Spencer Thorne held a woman and her two-year-old child hostage after carjacking them in Dalyellup, south of Bunbury, in WA's South West.

item.title: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa spared jail over child abuse material
item.description: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa is spared jail after a court found her novel fetishised young children.

item.title: NuGrow to fight charges of nuisance odours from Ipswich facility
item.description: Waste recycling company NuGrow will fight 11 environmental nuisance charges relating to its Swanbank food recycling plant in a two-week trial later this year.

item.title: Perth man David Moussa pleads not guilty to stabbing father to death on grounds of insanity
item.description: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Penny Wong to press Asian countries for fuel guarantees during regional tour
item.description: As Australia grapples with energy supply issues, Penny Wong travels to Japan to stress the importance of an ongoing supply of fuel.

item.title: CCTV footage shows the moment underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead
item.description: The Victorian Supreme Court has released footage of the moment underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead in Melbourne.

item.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
item.description: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
item.description: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

item.title: AFL's handling of players' mental health under scrutiny
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Saudi Arabia's Reality Check
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Superyacht linked to Putin's friend slips through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It's the most talked-about and dangerous stretch of ocean in the world but, at the weekend, a superyacht linked to one of Russia's richest men made it through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UAE leaves OPEC in major blow to global oil producers' group
item.description: It comes as unprecedented energy crisis triggered by the Iran war exposes splits among Gulf nations.

item.title: King Charles says 'acts of violence will never succeed' after attack on Trump — as it happened
item.description: King Charles has delivered a speech to Congress as part of his visit to the US to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
item.description: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
item.description: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

item.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on devices belonging to police killer Dezi Freeman
item.description: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

item.title: Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
item.description: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

item.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
item.description: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
item.description: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

item.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
item.description: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

item.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?
item.description: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
item.description: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

item.title: UAE exit from OPEC another disruption to the fragile oil market
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is set to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, creating yet another disruption to the global oil market. At home, Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the Middle East war is to blame for a rise in inflation.

item.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
item.description: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

item.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
item.description: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

item.title: What we know about the night 5yo girl Sharon Granites went missing in Alice Springs
item.description: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

item.title: King Charles addresses joint sitting of US Congress
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
item.description: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

item.title: Can the SA government justify a $45m golf course upgrade without LIV Golf?
item.description: The Malinauskas government took a calculated risk to redevelop the North Adelaide Golf Course in time for LIV Golf in 2028. It might not pay off.

item.title: Self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace barriers
item.description: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.

item.title: Paid advertisements used to promote fraudulent investment platforms on social media, warns ASIC
item.description: The Sydney dad believed he was investing in cryptocurrency, stocks and even gold with the hopes of buying a home.

item.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
item.description: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

item.title: Saba Vasefi says the Iranian regime has targeted her in Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Why WA's unique history has it leading the pack on ADHD medication prescriptions
item.description: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

item.title: Takeaways from King Charles's speech to the US Congress
item.description: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

item.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
item.description: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: Inflation rises to highest level since 2023, ASX drops for seventh consecutive day — as it happened
item.description: ABS data shows a rise in inflation to 4.6 per cent in March, the highest since September 2023, after another negative day for the ASX with the Reserve Bank widely expected to lift interest rates again next week. Look back on our live blog.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: Victorian opposition promises new cap on public sector executive pay
item.description: The Coalition is promising to cap the salaries of senior public sector executives at just under $600,000 a year to "restore financial discipline".

item.title: LIV Golf postpones United States event amid tour's uncertain future
item.description: LIV Golf and the US state of Louisiana make a call on the tour's event in New Orleans, which was slated for June.

item.title: UK parliament votes against inquiry into Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over Peter Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer criticised the vote, calling it a political stunt by the Conservative Party timed to sway voters ahead of local and regional elections on May 7.

item.title: Video of arson attack on Central Coast Pilates studio released by police
item.description: The video released by police captures the moment a man douses the Pilates studio in petrol before setting it alight.

item.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
item.description: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

item.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: Australian actor Megan Wilding stars in Game. Set. Match, a play that feels like tennis
item.description: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

item.title: Driving through bull dust
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jarome Luai's move to Papua New Guinea makes the power of the Chiefs very real
item.description: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

item.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
item.description: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

item.title: The search for missing girl Sharon Granites
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police reveal items seized from crime scene in missing 5yo investigation
item.description: Northern Territory Police say some people in the community "absolutely know" the whereabouts of five-year-old girl Sharon Granites's suspected abductor, and are imploring anyone with information that could help find them to come forward.

item.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted in probe over seashells photo
item.description: Former FBI director James Comey has been charged over a social media photo of seashells that officials say constituted a threat against US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
item.description: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Mother who faked son's eye cancer diagnosis to raise money sentenced to over four years' jail
item.description: A mother who faked her six-year-old son's cancer diagnosis to raise thousands of dollars has been jailed for more than four years for the "cruel, calculated" and "manipulative" crime.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
item.description: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

item.title: City of Sydney to investigate ban on short-term rentals
item.description: A Greens-led proposal passed by the council last night argues the current 180-day cap on short-term rental accommodation is not doing enough to address the rental housing crisis.

item.title: Health Minister says Canberra Hospital knife incident will be subject to a joint review
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

item.title: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March as higher fuel prices bite
item.description: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March, but underlying inflation remains at 3.3 per cent.

item.title: Australia's Travis Bazzana makes Major League Baseball debut for Guardians against Rays
item.description: As a classic Australian rock song rang out across the Cleveland Guardians' home ground, Travis Bazzana made his long-awaited MLB debut.

item.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
item.description: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

item.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
item.description: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

item.title: Childcare group G8 to close 40 centres after child abuse scandal
item.description: Victoria and Western Australia will be affected the most, with 12 centres shutting in each state. NSW, Queensland and South Australia will also have closures.

item.title: Robyn Lambley speaks on the search for Sharon Granites
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Northern Beaches Hospital returns to public ownership after death of Joe Massa
item.description: The NSW premier says a family's campaigning after their toddler died following treatment at Northern Beaches Hospital helped change public policy.

item.title: New South Wales still committed to saving Tomago Aluminium as negotiations stall
item.description: The NSW government remains committed to Tomago Aluminium but is still waiting to learn how much the bailout will cost.

item.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up an Australian Rules football club's name?
item.description: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

item.title: Brodie Dewar, accused killer of Labor identity Tim Picton, pleads not guilty to manslaughter
item.description: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Two men charged under Queensland's hate speech laws enter not guilty pleas
item.description: One of the men told the court: "I was charged with saying ‘From the River to the Sea,’ the great crime of the century".

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: What is OPEC? Why is the UAE leaving it?
item.description: The UAE is leaving an international oil-producers' group that has a major influence on the price of oil around the world.

item.title: AI deepfakes are circulating in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?
item.description: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

item.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
item.description: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

item.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
item.description: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

item.title: Head of CFMEU made threats and called government officials 'lazy c***s' at negotiation meeting, inquiry hears
item.description: The Commission of Inquiry into the CFMEU is in its eighth round of hearings and CPB Contractors general manager Vince Sanfilippo is in his second day giving evidence.

item.title: Plane crashes at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north
item.description: A small plane has crashed into a hangar causing a fire at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north.

item.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
item.description: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

item.title: Beau Lamarre-Condon's mother accused of intending to interfere with witness in Jesse Baird, Luke Davies murder case
item.description: The mother of a former police officer accused of killing Sydney couple Jesse Baird and Luke Davies has been charged after allegedly attempting to influence a key witness.

item.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
item.description: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

item.title: Illicit cigarettes, vapes, cash seized in raids on Canberra's black market tobacco trade
item.description: The ACT's chief police officer wants a sustained crackdown on the black market tobacco trade, as authorities swoop on six Canberra businesses.

item.title: Former Australian Olympic swimming team physio Peter John Wells to stand trial for alleged child sex offences
item.description: Peter John Wells, 55, pleaded not guilty to one count of sexual assault, and five counts of maintaining a sexual relationship with a child.

item.title: Light plane crashes at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
item.description: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

item.title: Goldfields defensive driving course helps teens cope with unique road challenges
item.description: A new defensive driving course being offered in Kalgoorlie aims to equip young motorists with the skills to cope with the unique challenges of outback driving.

item.title: Former police officer loses appeal over conviction for assaulting teens during arrest in Batemans Bay
item.description: A former NSW police officer has failed in an attempt to overturn a conviction for using unreasonable force in the arrest of two teenage boys.

item.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
item.description: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

item.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
item.description: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

item.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
item.description: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

item.title: Police explore link between alleged kidnappings and Melbourne hospitality attacks
item.description: Police say the trio are linked to targeted attacks on promoters of a weekly event held in South Melbourne.

item.title: More than 290 people honoured with police bravery awards for acts of courage during Wieambilla tragedy
item.description: Police officers and civilians have been honoured for acts of courage during the Wieambilla shootings — which left two officers and a neighbour dead — at a police ceremony in Dalby.

item.title: Donald Trump's criticism of Australia unfair and harmful, says US Democrat Joe Courtney
item.description: US congressman Joe Courtney says the US president's repeated criticism of Australia is "unfair" and causing harm to the US amongst its allies.

item.title: Two killed in light plane crash at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north
item.description: A pilot and a passenger die in a plane crash at Parafield Airport in Adelaide's north, which also caused injuries to several other people on the ground — including one with life-threatening burns.

item.title: Man accused of hit-and-run murder of ex-partner at Daisy Hill committed to stand trial
item.description: Tyler Scott Willian James has been charged with the murder of his ex-partner Kiesha Thompson, 23, who was struck by a vehicle in July, 2024.

item.title: War hero and veteran MP Bill Grayden dies aged 105
item.description: WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, is being remembered for his service to his country and community.

item.title: ABC News - Trusted Source of Latest News & Headlines
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Countries including Australia on the hunt for new sources of oil
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Missing girl Sharon Granites's family urges anyone with information to come forward
item.description: Family members of a missing Alice Springs five-year-old have spoken to the ABC for the first time, pleading for anyone with information that could help find their "energetic" little girl to come forward.

item.title: Trump can't keep getting 'free reign to conduct this war', says US Congressman
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
item.description: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

item.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
item.description: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

item.title: Penny Wong says Chinese Government working with Australian businesses on jet fuel supplies
item.description: Australia's foreign minister says the Chinese Government is engaging with Australian businesses on jet fuel supplies as she tours the region.

item.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

item.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
item.description: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

item.title: Back Roads: Navigating bull dust and racing the sun on the Binns Track
item.description: Journeying through the Binns Track in remote central Australia, Rae Johnston learns why you should always travel with mates.

item.title: Jeff Buckley doco It's Never Over humanises the man beyond the tortured myth
item.description: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

item.title: King Charles III visits September 11 memorial as he tours New York
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: New research uncovers 'missing' braincase and skull fragment of giant dinosaur Siamraptor in Thailand
item.description: Two fossils found in Thailand are named as long-missing pieces of a "shark-toothed" dinosaur after being identified partly by accident.

item.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
item.description: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.
